name: Tri-County Trust Company
description_short: Tri-County Trust Company is a community bank offering checking, savings, loans, and deposit products with a focus on local banking relationships.
description_long:
Tri-County Trust Company operates as a traditional community bank serving customers through multiple locations. Based on their website and organizational structure, they are a full-service financial institution rather than a specialized lender or fintech company. The company is led by President & CEO Shannon Damron and maintains a board of directors consisting of local business leaders including Tim Monnig as Chairman. Their routing number (101904225) and NMLS number (541725) confirm their status as a regulated financial institution.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and various loan products. They specifically highlight agricultural lending through their Ag Lender position, suggesting a focus on rural or agricultural communities. Recent service additions include GoStatements and GoNotices, an electronic statement and notice delivery system available at no cost to customers. They also offer Mastercard gift cards and pre-paid gift card products. Online banking is available through their GoBanking platform, which provides account access and statement management.

Tri-County Trust distinguishes itself as a community-focused institution with personalized service. Their staff directory prominently features individual loan officers and customer service representatives by name, suggesting relationship-based banking rather than automated or call-center-only operations. The presence of an agricultural lending specialist indicates specialization in underserved market segments. Their emphasis on secure electronic services and paperless options shows modernization while maintaining traditional community banking values.

The bank serves customers through multiple physical locations with published hours, indicating a traditional brick-and-mortar banking model. While specific product details like interest rates, loan terms, and account fees are not available on the provided website pages, customers can contact them directly at (660) 338-2234 during business hours. This is a regulated financial institution suitable for customers seeking traditional community banking relationships rather than specialized financial services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit cards with lost/stolen card reporting
Customer service by phone, fax, and email
Multiple branch locations with customer access
pros: Free electronic statements and notices (GoStatements/GoNotices) with 18 months of history available
Personalized service with named loan officers and staff available for relationship banking
Agricultural lending specialist on staff, indicating specialized support for farm/agricultural customers
Multiple physical branch locations with published hours for in-person banking
Online banking platform (GoBanking) for account access and management
FDIC-regulated financial institution with full banking license
Mastercard gift card and prepaid card offerings for diverse customer needs
cons: Limited product details available on website (rates, fees, and specific terms not disclosed)
Appears to serve primarily rural/regional areas based on Glasgow weather widget and agricultural focus
No evidence of competitive rates or promotional offers highlighted on website
Smaller institution may have fewer ATM locations compared to national banks
Website lacks detailed account comparison tools or rate information for informed shopping
best_for: Agricultural and farm business owners seeking specialized lending and banking relationships
Rural and small-town customers preferring local, personalized banking relationships
Customers seeking traditional community banking with in-person branch access
Depositors comfortable with smaller regional financial institutions
diagnosis: Tri-County Trust Company is best for rural and small-town customers seeking traditional community banking with personalized relationships and physical branch access. The main caveat is that this is a regional institution with limited online information about specific products, rates, and fees—prospective customers will need to contact them directly for detailed comparisons and pricing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
